actions.c(3) Library Functions Manual actions.c(3)

NAME

actions.c - Action option callbacks for ascii-chat.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void actions_defer (deferred_action_t action, const action_args_t *args)
deferred_action_t actions_get_deferred (void)
const action_args_t * actions_get_args (void)
void action_list_webcams (void)
void action_list_microphones (void)
void action_list_speakers (void)
void action_show_capabilities_immediate (void)
Execute show capabilities immediately (for early binary-level execution). void action_show_capabilities (void)
void action_check_update_immediate (void)
Execute update check immediately (for early binary-level execution). void action_check_update (void)
void action_show_version (void)
void action_help_server (void)
void action_help_client (void)
void action_help_mirror (void)
void action_help_acds (void)
void action_help_discovery (void)
void action_create_manpage (const char *output_path)
void action_create_config (const char *output_path)
void action_completions (const char *shell_name, const char *output_path)
void actions_execute_deferred (void)

Detailed Description

Action option callbacks for ascii-chat.

Action options are deferred until after all options are fully parsed and initialized. This ensures that options like --width and --height are properly reflected in action output (e.g., --show-capabilities displays the final terminal dimensions).

Examples: --list-webcams, --list-microphones, --list-speakers, --show-capabilities

Definition in file options/parsing/actions.c.

Function Documentation

void action_check_update (void )

Definition at line 347 of file options/parsing/actions.c.

347                                {
348   // Defer execution until after options are fully parsed
349   actions_defer(ACTION_CHECK_UPDATE, NULL);
350 }

void
  action_check_update_immediate (void )


Execute update check immediately (for early binary-level
    execution).


Definition at line 329 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


329                                          {
330   printf("Checking for updates...\n");
331   update_check_result_t result;
332   asciichat_error_t err = update_check_perform(&result);
333   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
334     printf("\nFailed to check for updates.\n\n");
335     action_exit(1);
336   }
337   if (result.update_available) {
338     char notification[1024];
339     update_check_format_notification(&result, notification, sizeof(notification));
340     printf("\n%s\n\n", notification);
341   } else {
342     printf("\nYou are already on the latest version: %s (%.8s)\n\n", result.current_version, result.current_sha);
343   }
344   action_exit(0);
345 }






References update_check_format_notification(), and
    update_check_perform().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_completions (const char * shell_name, const char *
  output_path)


Definition at line 589 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


589                                                                          {
590   if (!shell_name || strlen(shell_name) == 0) {
591     log_plain_stderr("Error: --completions requires shell name (bash, fish, zsh, powershell)");
592     action_exit(ERROR_USAGE);
593   }
594 
595   completion_format_t format = completions_parse_shell_name(shell_name);
596   if (format == COMPLETION_FORMAT_UNKNOWN) {
597     log_plain_stderr("Error: Unknown shell '%s' (supported: bash, fish, zsh, powershell)", shell_name);
598     action_exit(ERROR_USAGE);
599   }
600 
601   FILE *output = stdout;
602   bool should_close = false;
603 
604   // Determine output: use provided path if given and not "-", otherwise stdout
605   if (output_path && strlen(output_path) > 0 && strcmp(output_path, "-") != 0) {
606     // Check if file already exists and prompt for confirmation
607     struct stat st;
608     if (stat(output_path, &st) == 0) {
609       // File exists - ask user if they want to overwrite
610       log_plain("Completions file already exists: %s", output_path);
611 
612       bool overwrite = platform_prompt_yes_no("Overwrite", false); // Default to No
613       if (!overwrite) {
614         log_plain("Completions generation cancelled.");
615         action_exit(0);
616       }
617 
618       log_plain("Overwriting existing completions file...");
619     }
620 
621     output = platform_fopen("file_stream", output_path, "w");
622     if (!output) {
623       log_plain_stderr("Error: Failed to open %s for writing", output_path);
624       action_exit(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION);
625     }
626     should_close = true;
627   }
628 
629   asciichat_error_t result = completions_generate_for_shell(format, output);
630 
631   if (should_close) {
632     fclose(output);
633   }
634 
635   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
636     log_plain_stderr("Error: Failed to generate %s completions", completions_get_shell_name(format));
637     action_exit(ERROR_USAGE);
638   }
639 
640   // Silently exit - completions are written (either to file or stdout)
641   action_exit(0);
642 }






References completions_generate_for_shell(),
    completions_get_shell_name(), completions_parse_shell_name(),
    platform_fopen(), and platform_prompt_yes_no().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_create_config (const char * output_path)


Definition at line 542 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


542                                                    {
543   // Build the schema first so config_create_default can generate options from it
544   const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
545   if (unified_config) {
546     asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
547     if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
548       // Schema build failed, but continue anyway
549       (void)schema_build_result;
550     }
551     // options_config_destroy(unified_config);
552   }
553 
554   const char *config_path = NULL;
555 
556   // Determine output path: use provided path, or NULL for stdout
557   if (output_path && strlen(output_path) > 0 && strcmp(output_path, "-") != 0) {
558     // User provided explicit path - use it as-is
559     config_path = output_path;
560   } else {
561     // No path or "-" means stdout
562     config_path = NULL;
563   }
564 
565   // Create config with default options
566   asciichat_error_t result = config_create_default(config_path);
567   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
568     asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
569     if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
570       log_plain_stderr("Error creating config: %s", err_ctx.context_message);
571     } else {
572       log_plain_stderr("Error: Failed to create config file");
573     }
574     action_exit(ERROR_CONFIG);
575   }
576 
577   if (config_path) {
578     log_plain_stderr("Created default config file at: %s", config_path);
579   } else {
580     log_plain_stderr("Config written to stdout");
581   }
582   action_exit(0);
583 }






References config_create_default(),
    config_schema_build_from_configs(), and
    options_preset_unified().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_create_manpage (const char * output_path)


Definition at line 493 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


493                                                     {
494   // Get binary-level config
495   const options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
496 
497   if (!config) {
498     log_plain_stderr("Error: Failed to get binary options config");
499     action_exit(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION);
500   }
501 
502   // Determine output path: use provided path, or NULL for stdout
503   const char *path_to_use = NULL;
504   if (output_path && strlen(output_path) > 0 && strcmp(output_path, "-") != 0) {
505     // User provided explicit path - use it
506     path_to_use = output_path;
507   } else {
508     // No path or "-" means stdout
509     path_to_use = NULL;
510   }
511 
512   // Generate merged man page from embedded or filesystem resources
513   // Resources are loaded automatically based on build type:
514   // - Production (Release): From embedded binary data
515   // - Development (Debug): From filesystem files
516   asciichat_error_t err =
517       options_config_generate_manpage_merged(config, "ascii-chat", NULL, path_to_use, "Video chat in your terminal");
518 
519   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
520     asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
521     if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
522       log_plain_stderr("Error: %s", err_ctx.context_message);
523     } else {
524       log_plain_stderr("Error: Failed to generate man page");
525     }
526     action_exit(ERROR_FILE_OPERATION);
527   }
528 
529   if (path_to_use) {
530     log_plain_stderr("Man page written to: %s", path_to_use);
531   } else {
532     log_plain_stderr("Man page written to stdout");
533   }
534 
535   action_exit(0);
536 }






References options_config_generate_manpage_merged(), and
    options_preset_unified().

void
  action_show_capabilities (void )


Definition at line 316 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


316                                     {
317   // Defer execution until after options are fully parsed and dimensions updated
318   // This ensures --width and --height flags are properly reflected in the output
319   actions_defer(ACTION_SHOW_CAPABILITIES, NULL);

void
  action_show_capabilities_immediate (void )


Execute show capabilities immediately (for early binary-level
    execution).


Definition at line 246 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


246                                               {
247   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
248 
249   // Determine if we should use colored output
250   bool use_colors = terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO);
251 
252   // Override color level if colors should not be shown
253   if (!use_colors) {
254     caps.color_level = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
255     caps.color_count = 0;
256     caps.capabilities &= ~((uint32_t)(TERM_CAP_COLOR_TRUE | TERM_CAP_COLOR_256 | TERM_CAP_COLOR_16));
257   }
258 
259   // Use detected terminal size (immediate execution doesn't have parsed options yet)
260   terminal_size_t size;
261   terminal_get_size(&size);
262   unsigned short width = size.cols;
263   unsigned short height = size.rows;
264 
265   log_color_t label_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_GREY : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
266   log_color_t string_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_DEBUG : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
267   log_color_t good_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_INFO : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
268   log_color_t bad_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_ERROR : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
269   log_color_t number_color = use_colors ? LOG_COLOR_FATAL : LOG_COLOR_GREY;
270 
271   printf("%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, "Terminal Capabilities:"));
272 
273   char size_buf[64];
274   snprintf(size_buf, sizeof(size_buf), "%ux%u", width, height);
275   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Terminal Size"), colored_string(number_color, size_buf));
276 
277   const char *color_level_name = terminal_color_level_name(caps.color_level);
278   log_color_t color_level_color = (caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_NONE) ? bad_color : string_color;
279   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Color Level"),
280          colored_string(color_level_color, (char *)color_level_name));
281 
282   char colors_buf[64];
283   snprintf(colors_buf, sizeof(colors_buf), "%u", caps.color_count);
284   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Max Colors"), colored_string(number_color, colors_buf));
285 
286   log_color_t utf8_color = caps.utf8_support ? good_color : bad_color;
287   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "UTF-8 Support"),
288          colored_string(utf8_color, (char *)(caps.utf8_support ? "Yes" : "No")));
289 
290   const char *render_mode_str = "unknown";
291   if (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND) {
292     render_mode_str = "foreground";
293   } else if (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND) {
294     render_mode_str = "background";
295   } else if (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK) {
296     render_mode_str = "half-block";
297   }
298   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Render Mode"),
299          colored_string(string_color, (char *)render_mode_str));
300   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "TERM"), colored_string(string_color, (char *)caps.term_type));
301   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "COLORTERM"),
302          colored_string(string_color, (char *)(strlen(caps.colorterm) ? caps.colorterm : "(not set)")));
303 
304   log_color_t reliable_color = caps.detection_reliable ? good_color : bad_color;
305   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Detection Reliable"),
306          colored_string(reliable_color, (char *)(caps.detection_reliable ? "Yes" : "No")));
307 
308   char bitmask_buf[64];
309   snprintf(bitmask_buf, sizeof(bitmask_buf), "0x%08x", caps.capabilities);
310   printf("  %s: %s\n", colored_string(label_color, "Capabilities Bitmask"), colored_string(number_color, bitmask_buf));
311 
312   fflush(stdout);
313   action_exit(0);
314 }






References colored_string(),
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), terminal_get_size(), and
    terminal_should_color_output().


Referenced by options_init().






void
  action_show_version (void )


Definition at line 446 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


446                                {
447   bool use_colors = terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO);
448   if (use_colors) {
449     printf("%s %s (%s, %s)\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, "ascii-chat"),
450            colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, (char *)ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL),
451            colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, (char *)ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE),
452            colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, (char *)ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_DATE));
453   } else {
454     printf("ascii-chat %s (%s, %s)\n", ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL, ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_DATE);
455   }
456 
457   action_exit(0);
458 }

Referenced by main().






void
  actions_defer (deferred_action_t action, const action_args_t * args)


Definition at line 75 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


75                                                                         {
76   // Only remember the first action found
77   if (g_deferred_action_state.has_action) {
78     return; // Action already deferred, ignore subsequent actions
79   }
80 
81   g_deferred_action_state.action = action;
82   g_deferred_action_state.has_action = true;
83 
84   // Copy arguments if provided
85   if (args) {
86     memcpy(&g_deferred_action_state.args, args, sizeof(action_args_t));
87   } else {
88     memset(&g_deferred_action_state.args, 0, sizeof(action_args_t));
89   }
90 }






References action, and args.


Referenced by action_check_update(),
    action_list_microphones(), action_list_speakers(),
    action_list_webcams(), and action_show_capabilities().






void
  actions_execute_deferred (void )


Definition at line 680 of file
    options/parsing/actions.c.


680                                     {
681   deferred_action_t action = actions_get_deferred();
682 
683   switch (action) {
684   case ACTION_NONE:
685     // No action to execute
686     break;
687 
688   case ACTION_LIST_WEBCAMS:
689     execute_list_webcams();
690     break;
691 
692   case ACTION_LIST_MICROPHONES:
693     execute_list_microphones();
694     break;
695 
696   case ACTION_LIST_SPEAKERS:
697     execute_list_speakers();
698     break;
699 
700   case ACTION_SHOW_CAPABILITIES:
701     execute_show_capabilities();
702     break;
703 
704   case ACTION_CHECK_UPDATE:
705     execute_check_update();
706     break;
707 
708   default:
709     log_warn("Unknown deferred action: %d", action);
710     break;
711   }
712 }






References action, and actions_get_deferred().
